marketState: neutral, tilting bullish medium-term on weather/COT but short-term overbought/overvalued
summary: NG front trades at $3.245, about $0.32 above the 'now' fair-value estimate of $2.929 (upper-half of the $2.43-$3.43 band), and near-term technicals look stretched: RSI is 83.8 on the 5m and 74.1 on the 15m, with price above the upper Bollinger band on 5m/15m/1h, arguing for a near-term pullback. Weather demand is running well above normal (+11.6% vs 5y, rangePos 0.88 near the top of its range) but 24h momentum is already cooling (-8.4) and the 8-16 day outlook eases to +6.9%, suggesting the current heat-driven premium may fade. Medium-term the picture is more constructive: managed money is net short and sitting at just the 18th percentile of its 5-year range - an unusually stretched short position that argues for mean-reversion/short-covering risk - seasonality gives 63% odds of a higher price in 4 weeks (reliability is low), and projected fair value rises from $2.93 now to $3.07 by +21 days, narrowing the current overvaluation. Storage remains a genuine headwind (+175 Bcf vs 5y, this week's +87 Bcf build beat the +74 Bcf forecast, a bearish surprise), capping how far any rally extends. Net take: fade the short-term overbought/overvalued extension while favoring dips as a longer-term long given the COT and weather tailwinds.
